commit/metric/memory/status/description TSV format. Already have JSONL equivalent. Low extraction value. E0.

src/notebooklm/cli/skill.py ~280 lines. Reads SKILL.md from package data, stamps HTML comment `<!-- notebooklm-py vX.Y.Z -->` into frontmatter, writes to ~/.claude/skills/ and ~/.agents/skills/. Status subcommand reads stamp + warns on drift. hatchling force-include makes canonical source the package data. Directly retires cross_locale_config drift problem. Needs Node port + canonical-source location decision (main-branch vs separate repo). E2 effort, ~1 day.

SKILL.md lines ~94-127. Structured section splits every command into 'Run automatically (no confirmation)' vs 'Ask before running' with one-line reason per command. Retrofit candidate for add-debt, pr-review, session-end, audit-*. ~30 min per skill. No infrastructure change — just documentation pattern. Needs SKILL_AUTONOMY_RULES.md pattern doc. E1 effort, ~2-3 hours total.

candidate: Nightly RPC Health Check with Auto-Issues
notes: .github/workflows/rpc-health.yml + scripts/check_rpc_health.py. Verifies 35+ RPC method IDs round-trip correctly with Google, auto-creates GitHub issue with bug/rpc-breakage/automated labels on mismatch. Doesn't apply to Firebase (stable), but latent pattern for Claude tool API contract monitoring, MCP server health, Anthropic SDK drift, GitHub Actions API. E2 effort when target contract identified.

CLAUDE.md Pull Request Workflow section. Mandatory 4-step ambient workflow: monitor CI via gh pr checks -> read comments via gh api -> address + reply with commit SHA -> verify mergeStateStatus CLEAN. Contrasts with invoked /pr-review. Design question: ambient (always loaded) vs invoked (on demand). Candidate for CLAUDE.md Section 4a ~15 lines. E1 effort, ~30 min.

SKILL.md Common Workflows section. Workflow steps include literal Task(prompt='...', subagent_type='general-purpose') invocations that agents copy-paste. Example: 'Research to Podcast (Automated with Subagent)' embeds the exact subagent spawn syntax. Reduces agent cognitive load from design-the-task to fill-parameters. Retrofit for long-running/async skill workflows. E1 effort.

candidate: Open Skills Ecosystem Investigation (npx skills add)
notes: README references 'npx skills add teng-lin/notebooklm-py' fetching SKILL.md from GitHub via 'open skills ecosystem'. Unverified — could be real skill registry highly relevant to JASON-OS distribution model, or aspirational naming. Flag: determine if real before acting. 15-min web research + examine the npx skills CLI source. E1.

.github/workflows/validate_links.yml + Python requests-based link checker in scripts/. SBOM shows deps: requests, urllib3, certifi, idna, charset-normalizer. Transferable for any link-rot-sensitive artifact (docs/*, README, skill registry entries). ~100 LOC estimate. Quick Scan inference — not clone-inspected. E0.

Knowledge candidate, not code. 419K stars + 45K forks + 4572 watchers vs 2 commits/90 days + 1191 open issues + 14/15 PRs stuck at action_required + OpenSSF 2.3/10. Full lifecycle of a curated-list project: community momentum -> commercial capture (APILayer sponsorship precedes community index in README) -> approval bottleneck -> celebrity stagnation. Cautionary reference for JASON-OS skill registry / community distribution model design. E0.
